title: Apache Hadoop MapReduce Architecture
description:
Apache Hadoop MapReduce is a software framework for easily writing applications which process vast amounts of data (multi-terabyte data-sets) parallelly on large clusters (thousands of nodes) built ofcommodity hardware in a reliable, fault-tolerant manner.

MapReduce is based on the concept of dividing the input data into smaller chunks (input splits), and processing each chunk in parallel using a mapper and a reducer. The output of the mapper is then sorted and shuffled before being passed to the reducer.

The MapReduce framework provides a set of APIs for developers to write their own mappers and reducers, and handles the distribution and execution of the tasks across the cluster. It also provides fault-tolerance and recovery mechanisms to ensure that the tasks are completed even in the case of node failures.

MapReduce is widely used in big data analytics, data mining, and other data-intensive applications. It is an essential component of the Apache Hadoop ecosystem and is used by many organizations for processing large-scale data.
